target: DW-003 AAO: assess whether automated pipeline removal of -0.285σ data point constitutes a falsification event and whether our verdict needs updating
tldr_evidence: The poller flagged that on 2026-05-16 the dome's automated AI review pipeline removed the DW-003 AAO row showing -0.285σ (3/7 daily positives), which contradicts the dome's >+0.3σ 12wk mean prediction. Live-site fetch at 2026-05-17T13:54Z confirmed the -0.285σ row is BACK in the DW-003 table (2 entries: 2026-03-19 +0.780σ and 2026-05-17 -0.285σ). The removal was a transient artifact of the known race condition between the dome's daily AI review pipeline (which removes rows) and the data pull pipeline (which re-adds them) — first documented in the 2026-05-11 change report: 'DW-002/DW-003 rows removed by AI review then re-added by data pull (automation race condition artifact — no net content change).' NOAA daily AAO data verified through 2026-04-30 (no May data published yet); April 2026 monthly mean = -0.1252σ. Two-point mean of dome's logged entries (+0.780 + -0.285) / 2 = +0.2475σ, below the >+0.3σ threshold but with only 2 of the required 12 weeks of data.
tldr_verdict: NOT a falsification event. Three reasons: (1) the data removal was transient — the -0.285σ row is back on the live site; (2) DW-003's test criterion is a 12-week mean >+0.3σ, and with only 2 data points no meaningful statistical assessment is possible; (3) the dome's own display now correctly shows the negative value. Our verdict stays null — correct for an ongoing test with insufficient data. Notable secondary finding: the dome's automated monitor scores null/unavailable data as passing (28/28 scored domains at 100.0%), so even during the brief removal window the monitor score was unaffected — this is a systemic scoring property, not unique to DW-003. No patch to data/predictions.json needed: our_verdict=null is appropriate.
falsification_rationale: DW-003 test window is ongoing (no close date). Criterion requires 12wk mean >+0.3σ. Only 2 data points logged (2026-03-19 and 2026-05-17); 10+ more weeks of data needed before the 12wk mean is meaningful. The -0.285σ reading is below threshold for week 2, but: (a) the prior week was +0.780σ; (b) individual weekly readings are expected to oscillate; (c) the 12wk mean cannot be computed yet. The automated pipeline removal was transient (same race condition pattern as 2026-05-11). This does not meet falsification criteria.
monitoring_note: DW-003 race condition pattern is now documented twice (2026-05-11 and 2026-05-16). The automated AI review pipeline systematically removes data rows; the data pull pipeline re-adds them. Net effect: no sustained data suppression. However, during the removal window (typically hours) the dome's monitor treats the missing row as null = passing, inflating the 100.0% monitor score. This is inherent to the dome's pass/null scoring logic. Recommend poller continues watching DW-003 for: (a) 12-week milestone (approximately 2026-06-23 if weekly cadence holds); (b) whether sustained removals (not just race-condition transients) occur for consistently negative weeks.
